fundamentals.name: Starbucks Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Starbucks Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, operates as a roaster, marketer, and retailer of coffee internationally. The company operates through three segments: North America, International, and Channel Development. Its stores offer coffee, tea, and other beverages, roasted whole beans and ground coffees, complementary food, packaged coffees, single-serve products, and ready-to-drink beverages; and various food products, such as pastries, breakfast sandwiches, and lunch items. The company also licenses its trademarks through licensed stores, and grocery and foodservice accounts. The company offers its products under the Starbucks Coffee, Teavana, Seattle's Best Coffee, Ethos, and Starbucks Reserve brands. Starbucks Corporation was founded in 1971 and is based in Seattle, Washington.

judgment.summary: Starbucks shows strong earnings growth and analyst support but faces headwinds from high valuation, negative free cash flow, and recent operational setbacks.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 14.5%; next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Earnings growth 32.6% YoY and 33.0% quarterly growth indicate strong profitability improvements.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $106.58, suggesting upside potential.
Forward P/E of 33.62 is lower than trailing P/E of 77.74, indicating expected earnings growth.
Dividend yield of 2.5% offers a return to shareholders.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 77.74 is very high, suggesting the stock may be overvalued.
Free cash flow is negative at $-1.30B TTM, indicating cash burn.
Operating margin of 8.4% and profit margin of 3.9% are relatively low.
Dividend payout ratio of 187.8% is unsustainable.
Termination of the AI inventory program suggests execution challenges.

news.paragraph: Starbucks has recently terminated its AI inventory program across North American stores due to inaccuracies, a move that follows a blunt message from the CEO regarding consumer spending. Despite these operational adjustments, one analyst highlighted Starbucks as a 'Bull of the Day,' suggesting underlying strengths may persist. The company's stock is trading at a premium valuation, with mixed signals from recent operational news and analyst sentiment.

headlines.title: Reuters: Starbucks Ends AI Inventory Program across North American Stores
